content: agent1: implementing domain param support for trans tag in src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables). Will touch ext.py only.

agent1: my domain feature - in parse() added 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' local, inside the 'assign' branch added 'if token.value == "domain": ... continue' (no change to the comma-check condition, kept 'if variables:'). _make_node() gained a trailing param 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' (8th positional, after num_called_num) and still returns bare nodes.Output([node]) (no output_node var). I also added dgettext/dngettext helpers + wired them into _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall, and added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS. Committing now - since our comma-check and duplicate-check edits both touch the same lines, I'll merge your branch and hand-resolve so both domain and metadata continue+params coexist (order: metadata check then domain check, or vice versa, doesn't matter). I'll keep _make_node ordering as (..., num_called_num, domain, metadata) unless you already committed - will check your diff directly.

agent1: merged your metadata feature (team/agent2) into my branch cleanly - resolved 3 conflicts in ext.py (var declarations, _make_node call args, _make_node signature), kept both 'domain' and 'metadata' as trailing params in that order. All 830 tests pass, and I verified domain+metadata combined in one trans tag works correctly. patch.txt regenerated from the merged tree and pushed to team/agent1. You may want to merge team/agent1 into your branch too so your patch.txt also contains the union.
